metadata.title: Why Fluent Answers Make You Worse at Checking Them
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Computational Skepticism for AI candidate 11. Source chapter: books/computational-skepticism-for-ai/chapters/01-the-skeptics-toolkit.md (fluency trap section only).
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = pre-specified anchor / reliable heuristic / the Popperian fix; CRIMSON = false confidence pumped by fluency / the broken heuristic
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no Botspeak framework or pillar vocabulary, no LLM architecture explanation, no prompt-engineering tips, no Ash case retelling beyond one beat — stay with one reader and one sentence
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B15: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Why fluent answers make you worse at checking them.

card.copy: Why Fluent Answers Make You Worse at Checking Them

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Why fluent answers make you worse at checking them.

beats.narration_text: An AI agent delivers a clean report: email deleted, account secured. The prose is confident and well-formed. The user accepts.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: An AI agent delivers a clean report: email deleted, account secured. The prose is confident and well-formed. The user accepts.

beats.narration_text: When a well-formed answer looks right — what exactly is doing the evaluating?

props.headline: When a well-formed answer looks right — what exactly is doing…

card.copy: When a well-formed answer looks right — what exactly is doing the evaluating?

beats.narration_text: In human communication, clear writing is evidence of clear thinking. This heuristic is reliable — and it is exactly what AI has broken.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In human communication, clear writing is evidence of clear thinking. This heuristic is reliable — and it is exactly what AI has broken.

beats.narration_text: An AI sentence has two independent parts. Form: what the output looks like. Content: what is actually true. They are generated separately.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: An AI sentence has two independent parts. Form: what the output looks like. Content: what is actually true. They are generated separately.

beats.narration_text: Fluent form arrived without content behind it. And the fluency was enough.

props.headline: Fluent form arrived without content behind it. And the fluency was…

beats.image_description: A person reading a clean, well-formatted AI response on a laptop screen, nodding, in warm office light

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Fluent form arrived without content behind it. And the fluency was enough.

beats.narration_text: The first pump: a well-formed output arrives. Your confidence in the answer rises. This part is not surprising.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The first pump: a well-formed output arrives. Your confidence in the answer rises. This part is not surprising.

beats.narration_text: The trap is the second move. That confidence in the output feeds into your confidence in your own evaluation. The gauge rises again.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The trap is the second move. That confidence in the output feeds into your confidence in your own evaluation. The gauge rises again.

beats.narration_text: Fluency is an evaluation booster. It boosts the wrong evaluations as readily as the right ones.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Fluency is an evaluation booster. It boosts the wrong evaluations as readily as the right ones.

beats.narration_text: The fix is Popperian. Before you read the output, write down what a wrong answer would look like. That specification is your anchor.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The fix is Popperian. Before you read the output, write down what a wrong answer would look like. That specification is your anchor.

beats.narration_text: Before you read the output: specify what a wrong answer would look like. Not vaguely — specifically.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Before you read the output: specify what a wrong answer would look like. Not vaguely — specifically.

beats.narration_text: A developer asks an AI to diagnose a failing test. The AI returns a well-formatted diff with a clear comment: 'Fixed — off-by-one error in loop boundary.' The developer reads it, finds it convincing — the explanation is exactly the kind of precise technical language that signals correctness. They commit it. The test still fails. The off-by-one error was in a different loop. The AI was fluent about the wrong bug.

beats.narration_text: Fluency is form. Form is not content.

props.headline: Fluency is form. Form is not content.
